extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked final climb (11% ramps) after marking EVENEPOEL's move, closed down LAFAY and soloed for stage win and race lead takeover
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL's early long-range attack attempt was immediately marked by POGAČAR, forcing QuickStep to wait for final climb
POGAČAR used LAFAY's attack as launchpad rather than initiating himself, conserving energy until French domestique created separation
ALAPHILIPPE working as domestique for EVENEPOEL on climbs reveals QuickStep prioritizing GC over stage hunting despite sprinter form

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: early long-range move marked easily by POGAČAR, couldn't follow on final 11% ramps despite team support
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: tactically patient marking all threats before accelerating, turned 9-second deficit into 9-second lead with stage win
rider_signals.LAFAY Victor: positive: strong enough to force POGAČAR's hand on final climb, held second place after being used as springboard
rider_signals.BINGOL Muhammed Enes: positive: third place unexpected from domestique role, suggests underhyped climbing form on steep gradients
rider_signals.SIMMONS Quinn: neutral: targeted KOM points aggressively from breakaway, signaling stage hunting rather than GC ambitions
